verses.text: Led to be tempted, after forty days' fast Jesus answers the tempter, 'Man shall not live by bread alone, but by... the word of God.' Hunger met by Scripture. Christ overcomes temptation with the written word.

Led to be tempted, after forty days' fast Jesus answers the tempter, 'Man shall not live by bread alone, but by... the word of God.' Hunger met by Scripture. Christ overcomes temptation with the written word.
Set on the temple's pinnacle and bidden to leap, He answers, 'Thou shalt not tempt the Lord thy God.' No presuming. Faith does not test God by needless risk.
Set on the temple's pinnacle and bidden to leap, He answers, 'Thou shalt not tempt the Lord thy God.' No presuming. Faith does not test God by needless risk.
Set on the temple's pinnacle and bidden to leap, He answers, 'Thou shalt not tempt the Lord thy God.' No presuming. Faith does not test God by needless risk.

Shown the kingdoms and offered them for worship, He says, 'Get thee hence, Satan'; serve God only; and angels minister to Him. Victory won. The Son refuses the world's shortcut and the angels attend Him.

Hearing John was imprisoned, Jesus dwells in Capernaum, where 'the people which sat in darkness saw great light.' Light dawns. The Saviour brings His gospel to a darkened land.

From then He preaches, 'Repent: for the kingdom of heaven is at hand.' The same call. Christ takes up John's message and makes it His own.
By the sea He calls Peter and Andrew, 'Follow me, and I will make you fishers of men'; they leave their nets. Prompt obedience. At His word they forsake all to follow.
By the sea He calls Peter and Andrew, 'Follow me, and I will make you fishers of men'; they leave their nets. Prompt obedience. At His word they forsake all to follow.
By the sea He calls Peter and Andrew, 'Follow me, and I will make you fishers of men'; they leave their nets. Prompt obedience. At His word they forsake all to follow.
He calls James and John, who leave the ship and their father to follow Him. Costly call. To follow Christ may mean leaving even home behind.
He calls James and John, who leave the ship and their father to follow Him. Costly call. To follow Christ may mean leaving even home behind.
Jesus goes about teaching, preaching, and 'healing all manner of sickness'; great multitudes follow. Word and work. His power confirms the gospel He proclaims.
